ogTitle: South Yilgarn vs South Yunderup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in South Yilgarn and South Yunderup.
heading: South Yilgarn vs South Yunderup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between South Yilgarn, Western Australia and South Yunderup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: South Yilgarn has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to South Yunderup (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is South Yilgarn or South Yunderup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: South Yilgarn has a flood risk score of 48/100, while South Yunderup scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: South Yilgarn scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. South Yunderup scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in South Yilgarn or South Yunderup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. South Yilgarn has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
